task_completion.description: The function f(x) = sin(x) is defined and plotted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'f(x) = sin(x)' in the GeoGebra Algebra view (left sidebar) and the corresponding sine wave curve in the Graphics view.

task_completion.description: An integer slider 'n' is created for the number of rectangles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a slider named 'n' in the Algebra view or Graphics view. It should be set to an integer value (e.g., n = 5, n = 10) representing the number of subdivisions.

task_completion.description: The Upper Riemann Sum is calculated and linked to the slider 'n'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an 'UpperSum' command in the Algebra view (e.g., UpperSum(f, 0, π, n)) and/or overestimating rectangles drawn over the curve in the Graphics view.

task_completion.description: The Lower Riemann Sum is calculated and linked to the slider 'n'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'LowerSum' command in the Algebra view (e.g., LowerSum(f, 0, π, n)) and/or underestimating rectangles drawn under the curve in the Graphics view.

task_completion.description: The exact integral value is calculated for comparison.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an 'Integral' command in the Algebra view evaluating the function from 0 to π. The resulting value should be exactly 2 (or 2.0).

task_completion.description: A text annotation is added to display the area values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text box visible in the Graphics view that explicitly shows the numerical values of the Upper Sum, Lower Sum, and/or the exact Integral.

task_completion.description: The project is saved as 'riemann_sums.ggb' in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the filename 'riemann_sums.ggb' in the GeoGebra window title bar, or a file manager/terminal showing the file saved in '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GeoGebra GUI to create the applet.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GeoGebra application open with progressive additions to the Algebra and Graphics views, rather than just terminal commands.

integrity.description: The area values are genuinely computed by GeoGebra.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Algebra view should show the actual GeoGebra commands (UpperSum, LowerSum, Integral) generating the values, rather than the agent just typing static numbers into a text box.

integrity.description: The sums are dynamically linked to the slider.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UpperSum and LowerSum definitions in the Algebra view must use the variable 'n' (or the specific slider name) as their parameter for the number of rectangles, rather than a hardcoded number.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Function: f(x) = sin(x). Interval: [0, π]. Exact integral value: 2. Slider 'n' range: 1 to 50. Required commands: UpperSum, LowerSum, Integral. Expected save location: /home/ga/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/riemann_sums.ggb.
